sanskrit: मय्येव मन आधत्स्व मयि बुद्धिं निवेशय । निवसिष्यसि मय्येव अत ऊर्ध्वं न संशयः ॥
hindiTranslation: तू मेरेमें मनको लगा और मेरेमें ही बुद्धिको लगा इसके बाद तू मेरेमें ही निवास करेगा  इसमें संशय नहीं है ॥
englishTranslation: Fix your mind on me alone. Place your discerning mind in me, and you will live in me beyond doubt.
essence: A divided mind settles when both thought and feeling rest in the divine.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: Keep your mind and discerning mind fixed on Krishna. Then you will abide in him, with no doubt about the result.
simpleInsight: Single-pointed devotion quiets inner conflict and gives the heart a place to rest.
detailedMeaning: On the Kurukshetra battlefield, Arjuna is still shaken, and Krishna continues the teaching of bhakti. After promising rescue to those devoted to him, he now gives the inner method: place both mind and discernment on him alone.
modernRelevance: You are staring at your phone before sending a message that could change a relationship. Half of you wants control, half wants escape. This verse asks for one center, not two.
translationLiteral: मयि (in me) / एव (alone) / मनः (mind) / आधत्स्व (place) / मयि (in me) / बुद्धिम् (discerning mind) / निवेशय (fix) / निवसिष्यसि (you will dwell) / मयि (in me) / एव (alone) / अतः (therefore) / ऊर्ध्वम् (afterward) / न (not) / संशयः (doubt)
insight: It shifts attention from divided thinking to wholehearted devotion, so the mind stops scattering its power.
